title: TrackFlow — Frontend Implementation Plan for Full Habit Tracker with UPI Payment & Admin Panel

requirements.summary: Implement Internet Identity authentication flow with redirect to pricing page after login
acceptanceCriteria: Login page displays Internet Identity sign-in button
Successful login redirects to /pricing page
Unauthenticated users are redirected to login when accessing protected routes

file_operations.description: Create login page with Internet Identity authentication. Use the authorization component to handle login flow. Display the TrackFlow logo (frontend/public/assets/generated/trackflow-logo.dim_256x256.png) prominently. After successful login, redirect to /pricing. Include error handling for authentication failures.

Create ProtectedRoute wrapper component that checks authentication status using the authorization component. Redirect unauthenticated users to /login. Handle loading states while checking authentication.

Set up React Router with routes: /login (public), /pricing (protected), /dashboard (protected), /analytics (protected), /activities (protected), /habits (protected), /profile (protected), /admin (protected, admin-only). Wrap protected routes with ProtectedRoute component. Set /login as the default route for unauthenticated users.

Create custom hook to manage authentication state using the authorization component. Expose isAuthenticated, isAdmin, userProfile, login, logout, and loading states. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Create pricing/plan selection page with Free, Starter, and Premium plans
acceptanceCriteria: Pricing page shows all three plans with features listed
Free plan button takes user directly to dashboard without payment
Starter and Premium plan buttons navigate to the UPI payment page with the selected plan pre-filled
Pricing in INR (₹149/mo, ₹399/mo, ₹3,199/yr) is displayed correctly

file_operations.description: Create pricing page displaying three plan cards: Free (3 habits, basic checkbox analytics, no payment), Starter (₹149/month, 10 habits, normal analytics), and Premium (₹399/month or ₹3,199/year, unlimited habits, full analytics). Free plan button navigates to /dashboard. Paid plan buttons navigate to /payment with selected plan and cycle as URL params. Use Indigo (#4f46e5) primary color theme. Include feature comparison table.

Create reusable PlanCard component displaying plan name, price in INR, billing cycle, feature list, and CTA button. Apply consistent card styling with white background, subtle shadows, and hover transitions. Highlight recommended plan.

requirements.summary: Build UPI payment page with transaction ID submission and coupon validation
acceptanceCriteria: UPI ID yadavmannan007@okicici is prominently displayed
Transaction ID field is required before submission
Coupon code field applies a discount if a valid coupon is entered
Submitted payment is stored with status 'pending' in the backend
User sees a 'Payment submitted, awaiting verification' confirmation screen after submission

file_operations.description: Create payment page displaying selected plan details, UPI ID yadavmannan007@okicici in large text, QR code placeholder image (frontend/public/assets/generated/upi-qr-placeholder.dim_300x300.png), transaction ID input (required), coupon code input (optional) with validation button, and Submit button. On submit, call submitPaymentRequest with plan, cycle, transactionId, and couponCode. Show success confirmation with 'Payment submitted, awaiting verification' message and redirect to /dashboard after 3 seconds. Handle validation errors and display toast notifications.

Create custom hook for payment operations: submitPayment (calls submitPaymentRequest), validateCoupon (calls getCoupon and returns discount info), and getMyPaymentRequests. Use React Query for data fetching and mutations. Handle loading and error states.

Create CouponValidator component with input field, validation button, and discount display. Call getCoupon to validate and show discount percentage. Display validation status (valid/invalid/expired) with appropriate styling.

requirements.summary: Build Dashboard page with quick stats, Add Activity form, Today's Timeline, and Habit Progress Bars
acceptanceCriteria: All four stat cards display real data from the backend
Add Activity form validates required fields and submits successfully
Timeline updates immediately after adding/editing/deleting an activity
Progress bars reflect current vs. goal hours per habit

file_operations.description: Create dashboard page with welcome message showing today's date, four stat cards (Total Hours Today, Total Earnings Today ₹, Productive Hours, Most Active Habit), Add Activity form section, Today's Timeline section, and Habit Progress Bars section. Fetch activities and habits using getActivities and getHabits. Calculate stats from activities. Use responsive grid layout. Display loading skeletons while data is fetching.
Create StatsCard component displaying label, value, icon, and optional trend indicator. Use white card background with subtle shadow. Apply Indigo accent color for icons.

Create AddActivityForm with fields: habit dropdown (populated from user's habits) with 'Add new' option, custom activity name, start time picker, end time picker, duration (auto-calculated), productive checkbox, earnings input (₹), and notes textarea. On submit, call logActivity. Validate required fields. Show success toast on submission. Clear form after successful submission.

Create ActivityTimeline component displaying today's activities as cards sorted by time. Each card shows time range, activity name, duration, earnings in ₹, productive badge, and edit/delete icons. Clicking edit opens inline edit form. Clicking delete shows confirmation dialog then calls deleteActivity. Refresh timeline after mutations.

Create HabitProgressBar component showing habit name, current hours vs. goal hours, and progress bar visualization. Use habit color from backend. Display percentage completion. Show goal type (daily/weekly) label.

Create custom hook for activity operations: fetch activities (calls getActivities), log activity (calls logActivity), update activity (calls updateActivity), delete activity (calls deleteActivity). Use React Query with automatic refetching. Handle loading, error states, and optimistic updates.
Create custom hook to calculate dashboard stats from activities data: total hours today, total earnings today, productive hours, and most active habit. Return computed values with proper typing.

requirements.summary: Build Analytics page with charts and date range filtering based on user plan
acceptanceCriteria: Charts render with real data from the backend
Date range selector filters all charts and stats
Free plan users see only basic checkbox-style analytics and an upgrade prompt for charts
Starter and Premium users see full chart analytics

file_operations.description: Create analytics page with date range selector (Last 7 days, Last 30 days, This Month), conditional rendering based on user plan (check userProfile.plan). For Free plan: show basic checkbox-style analytics and upgrade prompt modal. For Starter/Premium: show Pie Chart (time distribution by activity), Bar Chart (daily earnings trend), Line Chart (productivity trend), and summary stats (average daily hours, total earnings, most productive day, top 3 activities). Fetch activities with getActivities filtered by date range. Use a charting library for visualizations.
Create DateRangeSelector component with preset options (Last 7 days, Last 30 days, This Month). Emit selected range to parent component. Apply active state styling.
Create BasicAnalytics component for Free plan users showing checkbox-style simple stats: total activities, total hours tracked, total earnings. Display upgrade prompt button linking to /pricing.
Create TimeDistributionChart component rendering a Pie Chart showing percentage breakdown of time spent per activity. Use Indigo color palette. Include legend.
Create EarningsTrendChart component rendering a Bar Chart showing daily earnings in ₹ over the selected date range. Use Indigo bars. Label axes clearly.
Create ProductivityTrendChart component rendering a Line Chart showing daily productive hours over the selected date range. Use Indigo line color with smooth curves.
Create SummaryStats component displaying calculated metrics: average daily hours, total earnings in ₹, most productive day (date + hours), and top 3 activities by time spent. Use card grid layout.

Create custom hook to compute analytics data from activities: time distribution per activity, daily earnings array, daily productivity array, average daily hours, most productive day, and top activities. Accept date range as parameter. Return computed analytics objects.

requirements.summary: Build Activities page with filtering, pagination, bulk delete, and CSV export
acceptanceCriteria: Filters correctly narrow the displayed activities
Pagination works correctly across pages
Bulk delete removes selected activities from the backend
CSV export downloads a valid file with all visible columns

file_operations.description: Create activities page with filter bar (date range picker, habit dropdown, productive/unproductive toggle), activities table/list with columns (Date, Activity, Duration, Earnings ₹, Productive, Actions), checkboxes for bulk selection, pagination controls (10 items per page), Bulk Delete button, and Export to CSV button. Fetch activities using getActivities with applied filters. Handle bulk delete by calling deleteActivity for each selected item. Generate CSV client-side and trigger download.

Create ActivitiesFilter component with date range picker, habit dropdown (populated from user's habits), and productive filter toggle. Emit filter changes to parent component. Apply Indigo accent colors.

Create ActivitiesTable component displaying activities data in table format with columns: checkbox, Date, Activity (custom name or habit name), Duration, Earnings ₹, Productive (badge), Actions (edit/delete icons). Support row selection for bulk operations. Include inline edit capability. Apply striped row styling for readability.
Create Pagination component with Previous/Next buttons, page number display, and page size selector. Emit page change events to parent. Disable buttons appropriately at boundaries.

Create utility function to convert activities array to CSV format and trigger browser download. Include all columns: Date, Activity, Duration, Earnings, Productive, Notes. Handle special characters and formatting.

requirements.summary: Build Habits page with CRUD operations, streak display, and plan limit enforcement
acceptanceCriteria: Habits list displays all habits with streak count
Add/Edit/Delete operations persist to the backend
Free plan blocks adding a 4th habit and shows upgrade modal
Starter plan blocks adding an 11th habit and shows upgrade modal

file_operations.description: Create habits page displaying user's habits as cards showing name, goal type, goal value, color badge, current streak count, and edit/delete actions. Include Add Habit button that opens modal form. Check plan limits before allowing add: Free (max 3), Starter (max 10), Premium (unlimited). Show upgrade modal when limit is exceeded. Fetch habits using getHabits. Handle create/update/delete with createHabit, updateHabit, deleteHabit.

Create HabitCard component displaying habit name, goal (daily/weekly + hours), color indicator, streak count with flame icon, and edit/delete action buttons. Use white card background with hover effects.

Create HabitFormModal component (create/edit mode) with fields: habit name, goal type dropdown (daily/weekly), goal value (hours), and color picker. On save, call createHabit or updateHabit. Validate required fields. Close modal on success with toast notification.

Create UpgradePlanModal component showing current plan limit reached message, benefits of upgrading, and CTA button linking to /pricing. Display appropriate message for Free (3 habit limit) or Starter (10 habit limit) users.

Create custom hook for habit operations: fetch habits (calls getHabits), create habit (calls createHabit with plan limit check), update habit (calls updateHabit), delete habit (calls deleteHabit with confirmation). Use React Query. Include plan limit validation logic.

requirements.summary: Build Profile page with editable info, account stats, and data export
acceptanceCriteria: Phone number edits are saved to the backend
Account statistics reflect real backend data
Delete account removes all user data after confirmation
Export downloads a file containing the user's activities and habits

file_operations.description: Create profile page displaying user info from getCallerUserProfile (name, email, plan, plan expiry), editable phone number field with save button, account statistics section (Member since, Total activities logged, Total tracked hours, Total earnings ₹), Delete Account button (shows confirmation dialog before calling deleteAccount and logging out), and Export My Data button (downloads JSON/CSV of user's activities and habits). Calculate stats from activities and habits data.
Create ProfileInfo component displaying user name, email, current plan badge, and plan expiry date if applicable. Show principal ID in small text. Apply card styling.
Create EditablePhone component with phone input field, edit/save buttons. On save, update userProfile.phone and call saveCallerUserProfile. Show success toast on save.

Create AccountStats component displaying four stat items: Member since (createdAt formatted), Total activities logged (count), Total tracked hours (sum), Total earnings ₹ (sum). Use grid layout with icons.

Create DeleteAccountDialog component with warning message, confirmation checkbox ('I understand this action is permanent'), and Confirm Delete button. On confirm, call deleteAccount, clear all cached data, and redirect to /login.

Create utility functions to export user data: exportActivitiesJSON, exportHabitsJSON, and exportAllDataCSV. Fetch all activities and habits, format into JSON/CSV, and trigger browser download with timestamped filename.

requirements.summary: Build Admin Panel with payment queue, user management, coupon management, and platform stats
acceptanceCriteria: Pending payment requests are listed with transaction IDs
Approve action sets user's plan and marks payment as verified
Reject action marks payment as rejected and notifies via UI
Admin can manually override any user's plan
Coupon creation form validates code uniqueness and saves to backend
Existing coupons are listed and can be deleted

file_operations.description: Create admin panel page (accessible at /admin) with four main sections: Pending Payments Queue, Users Table, Coupon Management, and Platform Stats. Check isCallerAdmin and redirect non-admins with access denied message. Fetch data using getPendingPaymentRequests, listUsers, listCoupons, and getPlatformStats. Use tabbed interface for clean organization.

Create PaymentsQueue component displaying pending payment requests in table format with columns: User Name (fetch from listUsers), Selected Plan, Billing Cycle, Transaction ID, Coupon Used, Submitted At, and Actions (Approve/Reject buttons). On approve, call approvePaymentRequest. On reject, call rejectPaymentRequest. Show confirmation dialogs for both actions. Refresh queue after mutations with success toasts.

Create UsersTable component displaying all users with columns: Name, Principal (truncated with copy button), Phone, Current Plan (badge), Signup Date, Last Login, and Actions (Change Plan dropdown). On plan change, call setUserPlan with selected plan and null expiry. Show confirmation dialog before changing plan. Include search/filter functionality by name or principal.

Create CouponManagement component with Create New Coupon form (code input, discount % input, usage limit input, expiry date picker, Create button) and Existing Coupons list (code, discount %, usage limit, used count, expiry, delete action). On create, validate code uniqueness client-side then call createCoupon. On delete, show confirmation then call deleteCoupon. Display success/error toasts for all operations.

Create PlatformStats component displaying four key metrics from getPlatformStats: Total Users, Total Activities, Total Habits, Total Payment Requests. Use large stat cards with icons. Refresh data periodically (every 30 seconds).

Create custom hook for admin operations: fetch pending payments (getPendingPaymentRequests), approve payment (approvePaymentRequest), reject payment (rejectPaymentRequest), fetch users (listUsers), set user plan (setUserPlan), fetch coupons (listCoupons), create coupon (createCoupon), delete coupon (deleteCoupon), fetch platform stats (getPlatformStats). Use React Query with proper cache invalidation.

requirements.summary: Implement collapsible sidebar navigation with role-based visibility and consistent theme
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar collapses to icons-only on toggle
Admin link is hidden for non-admin users
All pages use the Indigo primary color scheme consistently
Toast notifications appear for all async success/error actions
Loading skeletons display while data is being fetched
Footer appears on all pages with correct year

file_operations.description: Create Sidebar component with collapsible behavior (toggle between full-width and icons-only). Include navigation links with icons: Dashboard (Home icon, /dashboard), Analytics (Chart icon, /analytics), Activities (List icon, /activities), Habits (Checkbox icon, /habits), Profile (User icon, /profile). Conditionally render Admin link (Shield icon, /admin) only when isAdmin is true from useAuth hook. Apply Indigo (#4f46e5) accent color to active route. Include TrackFlow logo (frontend/public/assets/generated/trackflow-logo.dim_256x256.png) at top. Add collapse toggle button.

Create Layout component wrapping Sidebar and main content area. Include Footer component at bottom. Apply consistent spacing and responsive behavior. Adjust content area margin based on sidebar collapsed state.
Create Footer component displaying '© {currentYear} TrackFlow. All rights reserved.' text centered with subtle gray styling. Use current year dynamically.

Create Toast notification component system (success, error, info variants) with auto-dismiss after 3 seconds. Use Indigo for success, red for error. Position fixed at top-right. Implement using context provider for global access.
Create LoadingSkeleton component with variants for cards, tables, and charts. Use subtle gray shimmer animation. Provide size props (small, medium, large) for flexibility.
Create ToastContext and ToastProvider to manage toast notifications globally. Expose showToast function accepting message, type, and duration. Maintain toast queue with auto-dismiss timers.
Wrap application with Layout component for protected routes. Include ToastProvider at root level. Ensure /login route does not use Layout wrapper.

requirements.summary: Apply clean, modern productivity-app visual theme with Indigo primary color
acceptanceCriteria: Consistent color palette applied across all pages
Typography is uniform using Inter or Poppins
Interactive elements have clear hover and focus states

file_operations.description: Update global styles with Indigo (#4f46e5) as primary color, white for card backgrounds, subtle grays for surfaces. Set Inter or Poppins as base font family. Define CSS custom properties for colors: --primary-indigo, --surface-gray, --card-white. Add smooth transition utilities for micro-interactions. Ensure generous spacing with consistent padding/margin scale.

Extend Tailwind theme with Indigo primary color (#4f46e5), custom gray shades for surfaces, Inter and Poppins font families. Configure hover and focus state variants for all interactive elements. Add custom spacing scale for generous whitespace. Define transition timings for smooth animations.
